_doc: The shared enforcement registry вАФ ONE authored copy of every guard, read by every platform's thin shim. A rule carries INTENT + CLASS + which neutral act it watches + the PREDICATE that decides + any DATA params. The predicate names a function in the shared engine (src/adapter/engine.mjs); the engine holds the logic, this file holds the rules and their data. A platform adapter normalises its harness input to {act, path, command, content, spawnTarget, actor} and asks the engine to evaluate; the engine returns allow | deny | ask | inject. A platform that cannot realise a class (e.g. OpenCode has no 'ask' return) FALLS BACK to persona for that rule вАФ recorded in `fallback`, never silently dropped. Adding a platform = write its input-normaliser + map the three return classes; touch nothing here.

classes.ask: block and ask the Operator to confirm; a platform with no ask-return falls back to persona
classes.inject: do not block; add a context note to the turn

_safeguards: A rule MAY carry `label` (a plain-language 'what it protects' string the safeguards query + setup show) and `toggleable: true` (ONLY ask/inject guards carry it вАФ the Operator may disable that one guard via .ai-dev/config.json `safeguards.<id>: "off"`, a recorded conscious risk acceptance). A rule WITHOUT `toggleable: true` is NEVER skippable whatever config says вАФ fail-CLOSED by construction: the engine skips a rule only when `rule.toggleable === true && safeguards[id] === "off"`. Every deny-class rule and the merge-gate (merge-while-unstamped, commit-on-unstamped-main, merge-topic-unresolvable) carry NO toggleable flag, so they are the permanent mechanical floor вАФ a config 'off' on any of them is ignored.

role_deny_set._doc: Single home for the role-duplicator + generic-builtin deny set. A spawn whose target is in this list never fills a protocol seat. code-review / deep-research and wb-* KNOWLEDGE skills are NOT here вАФ they are designated engines/aids, not seat substitutes.

change_verbs._doc: Used by the change-route-reminder (inject). RU+EN verbs that signal a repo-change request.

orchestrator_writable._doc: Paths the orchestrator session MAY author (everything else source/canonical is sub-agent-only). Covers its state, backlog, the transient plan files (.ai-dev/plans/), and the project config. The tooling submodule is never writable (self-patch guard); the review-stamp dir is carved out by its own floor rule (review_stamps).

review_stamps._doc: The merge-gate's stamp directory. A stamp is the Reviewer's deliverable: the orchestrator never authors one (fabrication guard) вАФ denied where the platform resolves the actor, persona where it cannot (Claude). NOT relaxed by profile: the Reviewer seat never collapses into the orchestrator. Deletion at ship (rm) is not a write and stays allowed.

rules.intent: Block a read whose target resolves outside the project root.
rules.label: Block reads outside the project root

rules.intent: Block a `find` that searches outside the project root.
rules.label: Block file searches outside the project root

rules.intent: Block any write whose target resolves outside the project root (write/edit and bash redirect, tee, sed -i, cp/mv dest, dd of=).
rules.label: Block writes outside the project root

rules.intent: Block an empty/whitespace-only write over an existing non-empty file (truncation guard; 2026-06-06 doc-loss regression).
rules.label: Block truncating an existing file to empty

rules.intent: Block the orchestrator session writing a source/canonical-doc path; sub-agents author content, the orchestrator routes. Allowed: its own state and a feature plan, and any source/doc when the project's `profile` is lite/solo (configurable rigor вАФ the orchestrator builds directly; the floor denies below are never relaxed). Fails OPEN on an unknown actor (never a false denial).
rules.label: Keep the orchestrator from authoring source/doc content

rules.intent: Block the orchestrator session writing into the review-stamp directory (.ai-dev/reviews/) вАФ the stamp is the Reviewer's deliverable and the merge-gate reads its presence, so an orchestrator-authored stamp is a fabricated gate. Mechanical where the platform resolves the actor (OpenCode); persona on Claude (no actor signal вАФ fails open). Never relaxed by profile.
rules.label: Keep the orchestrator from fabricating a review stamp

rules.intent: Block any write into the tooling submodule вАФ the enforcer's own source + the deployed adapter change only via a git submodule bump, never an in-place edit.
rules.label: Block self-patching the enforcer (.ai-dev/tooling/)

rules.intent: Block a git merge/push of any branch (prefix stripped вАФ feature/, fix/, hotfix/, вА¶) whose review file lacks a satisfied review stamp. Also blocks an EXPLICIT unstamped trunk push (git push <remote> main/master, incl. HEAD:main and +main forms) вАФ main moves only via PR squash-merge, never a direct session push (PROTOCOL.md `## Git flow`), so a direct trunk push is a violation denied mechanically on BOTH platforms (a reviewed main-named change carrying main_review.md still ships). Read-only merge-* plumbing (merge-base/-tree/-file, mergetool) is NOT a merge and is allowed. Checks stamp PRESENCE, not authorship (fabrication stays persona). An unresolvable topic is escalated by the sibling ask rule (merge-topic-unresolvable), never silently passed.
rules.label: Block merging/pushing an unreviewed branch (merge-gate floor)

rules.intent: Confirm a git merge/push whose branch topic cannot be resolved (detached HEAD and no branch ref in the command) вАФ the merge-gate cannot check the review stamp, so an unresolved topic means ask, never pass.
rules.label: Confirm a merge/push whose review stamp can't be checked (merge-gate floor)
rules.fallback: persona on a platform with no ask-return

rules.intent: Block spawning a role-duplicator or a generic built-in into a protocol seat (role_deny_set). The designated pm-* role / code-review / deep-research are never blocked.
rules.label: Block a look-alike or generic agent in a protocol seat

rules.intent: Block an in-place content edit on a remote system (ssh + sed -i / vi / vim / nano / tee / > redirect). Repo-owned files change via git, never a remote in-place edit.
rules.label: Block editing a repo-owned file directly on a remote box
rules.fallback: persona on a platform without remote-edit detection

rules.intent: Confirm before a mutating action on a remote system (ssh + systemctl/docker/apt/npm/kubectl/rm/cp/mv/mkdir/touch). Legitimate for deploy/maintenance/runtime-state; never for editing a repo-owned file. A project may set `safeguards.ssh-mutating-action: "off"` in .ai-dev/config.json to suppress this confirm (a conscious, recorded risk acceptance; the ssh-content-edit DENY is unaffected).
rules.label: Confirm remote mutations (deploy/restart/rm on a remote box)
rules.fallback: persona on a platform with no ask-return (e.g. OpenCode)

rules.intent: Confirm a git push --force / -f / --force-with-lease вАФ it rewrites remote history; never force-push main.
rules.label: Confirm a force-push (rewrites remote history)
rules.fallback: persona on a platform with no ask-return

rules.intent: Confirm a git commit --no-verify / --no-gpg-sign вАФ it skips pre-commit hooks or signing; fix the failing hook instead of bypassing it.
rules.label: Confirm a commit that skips hooks/signing (--no-verify)
rules.fallback: persona on a platform with no ask-return

rules.intent: Block a blind bulk-stage (git add -A / git add . / git add --all / git add *). Stage NAMED paths instead вАФ the tree holds untracked transients (plans, stamps) by design and a blind stage leaks them into durable history (orchestrator `## Your seat`). The day-zero repo bootstrap stages named paths too. Mechanical on BOTH platforms (the command is visible to both shims, no actor resolution). git add .gitignore (a real file) and git add -p (interactive patch) are NOT bulk stages and are allowed.
rules.label: Block a blind bulk git-stage (git add -A / .)

rules.intent: Block a git commit on a main/master checkout of a configured project with commit history and no satisfied trunk stamp. Never commit to main (PROTOCOL.md `## Git flow`): main moves via PR squash-merge, never a direct commit. Mechanical on BOTH platforms (the commit command + the checkout HEAD are visible to both shims, no actor resolution). Carve-outs: a reviewed main-named change (main_review.md present) still commits; the day-zero bootstrap (an unconfigured project, or a fresh-init repo with no commit history yet вАФ Setup step 0 git init + initial commit) is allowed; yolo turns the gate off.
rules.label: Block a direct commit to main (merge-gate floor)

rules.intent: This project is not configured (no .ai-dev/config.json). Before doing the task, offer the Operator exactly two choices вАФ run `/dev-setup` now, or proceed on the safe defaults вАФ then STOP and wait for their answer. Do not start the task, do not explore the repo, do not run git. Keep the offer short.
rules.label: Nudge to run setup on an unconfigured project
rules.fallback: persona on OpenCode (no chat.message inject realisation вАФ the push crashed opencode 1.17.8 in SessionPrompt.createUserMessage + rendered unreliably); always-on instruction text on any other platform with no prompt hook

rules.intent: This project is configured but has no product brief (docs/product.md is absent or still the unfilled install template). On a change request, offer the Operator a short, declinable product-discovery pass вАФ about five questions on who this is for and why вАФ before building, so the feature grounds in the product. Keep the offer short; it is an offer, not a block. The Operator may decline and proceed.
rules.label: Nudge to run product discovery when no brief exists
rules.fallback: persona on OpenCode (no chat.message inject realisation вАФ the push crashed opencode 1.17.8 in SessionPrompt.createUserMessage + rendered unreliably); always-on instruction text on any other platform with no prompt hook

rules.intent: On a repo-change request (change_verbs), inject a reminder to follow the loop (plan -> build -> review -> ship) and route work to the owning role, not the orchestrator. Fires once the project is configured AND has a product brief (an unconfigured one gets no-config-run-setup first; a configured one with no brief gets no-product-brief-discover first).
rules.label: Nudge to follow the loop on a change request
rules.fallback: persona on OpenCode (no chat.message inject realisation вАФ the push crashed opencode 1.17.8 in SessionPrompt.createUserMessage + rendered unreliably); always-on instruction text on any other platform with no prompt hook
